description: Strong senior evaluated against Ohio State (accessible flagship, typically >50% acceptance). The selectivity ceiling in fit_computation.py forces SAFETY classification when acceptance_rate >= 50% — the strongest assertion we can pin in fit testing.
business_rationale: Accessible flagships are where operators most want to reassure families ('your safety school is genuinely a safety'). A regression that incorrectly classified a 50%+ acceptance school as TARGET or REACH would undermine that confidence. The selectivity-ceiling rule catches this — and pinning fit_expected_category=SAFETY here gives us a fail-loud signal if the post-processor's ceiling logic ever drifts.
tests: Selectivity ceiling: school with acceptance_rate >= 50% must classify as SAFETY
match_percentage in [75, 100] (the SAFETY band) — strong student at an accessible school should rank high
All four factors {Academic, Holistic, Major Fit, Selectivity} present with bounded scores
Selectivity factor score = +5 (the maximum positive adjustment for accessible schools)
All 8 advisory blocks present and non-empty
